steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The system consists of a downward-opening parabola and a horizontal line at $y=6$. The solutions are the $x$-values where the two graphs intersect. Since the answer choices are single values, you only need to find one valid intersection $x$-coordinate.

steps.title: Read the intersection from the graph
steps.content: Trace the horizontal line $y=6$ until it meets the parabola. One intersection is directly above $x=4$ on the grid. That means $x=4$ is a solution of the system.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A gives $x=4$, which is one of the intersection $x$-values. The other choices are not located at an intersection with the horizontal line $y=6$. Therefore choice A is correct.
